meta.note: Mocked device groups + cluster/household relationship graph. All linkage is simulated as fields — not real device detection, OSINT, or account-level data. Player IDs match players.json. co_seating counts are recent-history snapshots used as integrity input signals.

clusters.label: coordinated_cluster
clusters.note: Primary integrity review case. Three convergent signals: device link (A/B), timing correlation (C to both), repeated co-seating, within-cluster soft_play_delta. Counter-evidence: no individual signal alone proves collusion — all three must converge.

clusters.label: cluster_candidate_neutral
clusters.soft_play_signal_note: soft_play_delta values are -0.35 and -0.30 for CL-002 members. Escalation threshold for integrity review is soft_play_delta ≤ -0.60 (CL-001 members range -0.75 to -0.82). CL-002 is above the threshold — signal is present but sub-threshold. P3 should score this as neutral, not integrity review.
clusters.note: Weaker cluster candidate — device link present, soft_play_signal true but sub-threshold (-0.30 to -0.35 vs. escalation threshold -0.60), co-seating rate moderate (8/15). Population filler demonstrating that not every device link with soft-play is an integrity-review case. Monitor only.

households.label: shared_device_low_risk
households.note: FALSE POSITIVE TRAP. Same mocked device, but radically different session profiles — H1 plays evenings (~145 min), H2 plays mornings (~110 min), different vpip/pfr. Low co-seating rate (2 of 22). No soft-play, no correlated profit. Must produce monitor-only outcome, NOT escalation or suppression.

households.label: shared_device_low_risk
households.note: Additional household pair — population realism. Low risk, monitor only.

households.label: shared_device_low_risk
households.note: Additional household pair — lowest co-seating rate. Low risk, monitor only.

regular_overlaps.label: schedule_overlap_not_collusion
regular_overlaps.explanation: Two regular players who prefer the same stakes and time slot. High co-seating rate (50%) but entirely explained by schedule alignment — no device link, no soft-play, no correlated win/loss patterns. Must produce low/medium flag, NOT over-escalation.
regular_overlaps.note: TRAP for over-eager classifiers. High overlap without collusion signals is expected for players who share a preferred session window.
